title: parser Subexp frame perf (was jssm#717)
manager: Optimize the parser's Subexp frame handling — the per-subexpression bookkeeping that the PEG parser pays on every parse. Parse cost is paid on construction from source (the common entry point), so shaving the Subexp frame is a lever felt by anyone who builds machines from strings.
cs: parse-path lever; recall the --cache memoization dead-end (3-17× slower) — the cliff is the parse itself
